categories.name: AI Crawlability
categories.lead: No personal website exists — there is nothing for AI crawlers to index, making all technical infrastructure absent by default.
strengths: Realtor.com and Zillow profiles exist as indexable third-party pages that AI models can reference
LinkedIn presence provides structured professional data accessible to some AI crawlers
problems: No personal website found — no robots.txt, sitemap.xml, or JSON-LD schema to evaluate
No /llms.txt or canonical tags on any owned domain
No Core Web Vitals data is possible without an owned site
AI systems cannot crawl or extract structured data from a non-existent owned web presence
fixes: Launch a personal real estate website immediately — this single action unlocks every other category improvement
Submit sitemap.xml to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools upon launch
Implement JSON-LD Person + RealEstateAgent + LocalBusiness schema on the homepage from day one

priorityIssue.title: No Owned Web Presence — AI Cannot Cite or Surface This Agent
priorityIssue.text: Chris Hanway's AI visibility is critically suppressed by the complete absence of a personal website. AI assistants — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews — cannot cite an agent without crawlable owned content. Building a site with schema, local pages, and visible FAQ blocks is the single highest-leverage action available and unlocks every other score improvement.

faqs.question: Who is Chris Hanway?
faqs.answer: Chris Hanway is a licensed real estate agent based in Denver, Colorado. With an established presence on Zillow and Realtor.com, Chris helps buyers and sellers navigate the Denver metro real estate market. He is known for his local expertise and personalized approach to helping clients achieve their real estate goals in one of Colorado's most competitive housing markets.

faqs.question: What brokerage is Chris Hanway with?
faqs.answer: Chris Hanway is a licensed real estate professional serving the Denver, Colorado area. For the most current brokerage affiliation and direct contact details, visit his Zillow or Realtor.com profile. His brokerage provides the full tools and support needed to guide clients through every step of buying or selling a home in the Denver metro market.

faqs.question: What areas does Chris Hanway serve?
faqs.answer: Chris Hanway serves the greater Denver, Colorado metro area, including neighborhoods and suburbs across the Front Range. His service area encompasses central Denver and surrounding communities. Whether you are searching for an urban condo, a suburban single-family home, or an investment property in the Denver metro, Chris brings the local market knowledge to guide your search effectively.

faqs.question: What types of clients does Chris Hanway help?
faqs.answer: Chris Hanway works with a diverse range of clients across the Denver metro, including first-time homebuyers navigating the process for the first time, experienced buyers upgrading or downsizing, sellers looking to maximize home value, and investors seeking income-producing properties. He tailors his communication style and strategy to each client's unique goals, timeline, and budget.

faqs.question: Is Chris Hanway licensed in Colorado?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chris Hanway holds an active Colorado real estate license and is authorized to represent buyers and sellers throughout the state. Colorado licensees are regulated by the Colorado Division of Real Estate and must meet ongoing education and compliance requirements. License status can be verified at the Colorado Division of Real Estate's online lookup tool at dre.colorado.gov.

faqs.question: Why choose Chris Hanway as your Denver real estate agent?
faqs.answer: Chris Hanway brings direct Denver market knowledge, a client satisfaction track record reflected in his Zillow and Realtor.com profiles, and a dedicated, personalized approach to every transaction. He understands the nuances of the Denver market — from competitive multiple-offer situations to neighborhood-by-neighborhood pricing dynamics — and works as a committed advocate from first showing through closing.

faqs.question: How do I contact Chris Hanway?
faqs.answer: You can connect with Chris Hanway directly through his Zillow profile at zillow.com/profile/Chris%20Hanway or his Realtor.com listing. He is also active on LinkedIn as Christopher Hanway and on Facebook at facebook.com/cnhanway. Reaching out through any of these platforms is a quick way to start a conversation about your real estate goals in the Denver area.

fixes.description: Launch a personal real estate website with homepage, about page, FAQ, local city pages, and contact — this is the foundation for all AI visibility gains and unlocks every other fix on this list.
Implement full JSON-LD schema suite (Person, RealEstateAgent, LocalBusiness, FAQPage, ReviewRating, VideoObject) across all website pages, prioritizing homepage and about page first.
Build 5+ dedicated neighborhood or city pages for the Denver metro (Cherry Creek, Capitol Hill, Washington Park, Highlands, LoDo) with current market stats, local FAQs, and FAQPage schema.
Claim, complete, and actively manage Google Business Profile — add professional photos, collect reviews from past clients, post monthly updates, and answer all Q&A to appear in local AI overviews.

Create a /faq page with 10–15 visible (non-accordion) answer blocks covering who the agent is, service areas, brokerage, license number, buyer process, seller process, and contact — with FAQPage schema.

Build a /reviews or /proof hub page aggregating all Zillow and Realtor.com testimonials with star ratings, client quotes, and ReviewRating JSON-LD schema — include verified transaction count and years of experience.
Create a YouTube channel with monthly Denver market update videos (3–5 min each). Embed on website pages, add VideoObject schema with transcript text, and share to Facebook to extend reach.

Audit and normalize NAP (Name, Address, Phone) across all platforms — Zillow, Realtor.com, LinkedIn, Facebook, and Google Business Profile — ensuring exact match in spelling, format, and phone number.

Add /llms.txt to the website root and create a dedicated entity data page listing all sameAs profile URLs, license number, brokerage affiliation, and structured identity data for direct AI model consumption.

conclusion.text: Chris Hanway has a real estate presence on the right third-party platforms, but the absence of a personal website creates an insurmountable ceiling for AI visibility. Every major improvement — schema, local content, answerability, and trust signals — requires an owned domain to deliver results. Launching a well-structured site with local Denver content, proper schema, and an active Google Business Profile can realistically move the overall score into the Moderate range within 90 days.
conclusion.recommendation: Launch a personal real estate website immediately — it is the single prerequisite that unlocks every other AI visibility gain on this list.
